title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
summary: PostHog built an automated pipeline that detects product signals like rage clicks and errors, groups them using LLM-generated embeddings, and generates pull requests to fix issues. The system uses background agents to analyze related errors, session replays, and codebase context, reducing manual triage and development cycles.
writeup: Joshua Snyder of PostHog presents an automated pipeline that turns product signals into code fixes. The system detects signals like rage clicks and errors, clusters them with LLM-generated embeddings, and dispatches background agents that analyze related errors, session replays, and codebase context to generate pull requests. The result reduces manual triage and shortens development cycles, demonstrating a path from raw product telemetry to proposed patches without a human opening every ticket.
